title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · Operations Case: Symbolic Octal Modes
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux symbolic octal modes increment in a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate symbolic and octal chmod operations while preserving unrelated bits and verifying the result.
Calculate read, write, and execute effects for owner, group, and other on regular files and directories.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

# Worked focus: linux-symbolic-octal-modes — Translate symbolic and octal chmod operations while preserving unrelated bits and verifying the result.

umask

# Derive directory create/delete behavior before any chmod or ACL change.
